root_cause.explanation: Profile publishing in subscribeNewGroups() only fires once per group via the onHistorySynced callback. This means when a new member joins a group (User B joins User A's group), User A's profile is NOT republished. User B only sees User A's profile if it was published in a historical MLS application message that was relayed and replayed. If User A has never published, or published a stale profile, User B sees nothing or outdated data. The onMembersChanged callback in MarmotContext.tsx only updates the memberPubkeys in storage — it does not trigger a profile republish to the group.
sequence: User A creates group; onHistorySynced fires and publishes A's profile to the group once; profilePublishedRef.current marks group as published
User B receives Welcome, joins group, and opens group detail view
User B's client fetches historical kind 445 events and ingests them via subscribeToGroupMessages()
User A's profile may appear in history if published early enough — but any profile change since then is invisible to User B

When User B joins, the MLS commit advancing the epoch fires onMembersChanged on User A's client — but current onMembersChanged handler only updates memberPubkeys in storage, never republishes A's profile
User B never receives a current profile from User A unless User A manually triggers publishProfileUpdate()

evidence.description: profilePublishedRef guards onHistorySynced to publish exactly once per group — no mechanism to re-publish when new members arrive
onMembersChanged callback only calls persistGroup and reloadGroups — no profile republish triggered
onHistorySynced callback publishes profile but is gated by profilePublishedRef — it runs exactly once and never again for that group

onMembersChanged fires after EVERY event ingest (commits, proposals) by calling getGroupMembers — including the commit that adds a new member. Epoch is already updated at this point, so sendApplicationRumor is safe.
10-second waitForTimeout workaround inserted to compensate for the missing republish — evidence of the bug's real-world impact

changes.change: Track the previous member count in a closure variable (initialized from the MLS member list before subscribing). In onMembersChanged, compare newMembers.length to prevMemberCount. If newMembers.length > prevMemberCount (i.e. members were added), republish the local profile to this group via sendApplicationRumor. Always update prevMemberCount to newMembers.length after the comparison. Do NOT gate this on profilePublishedRef — it must always re-publish when new members arrive regardless of whether it fired once before.
changes.reason: When onMembersChanged fires with a larger member list, the commit adding the new member has already been ingested and the local MLS epoch is current. It is safe to call sendApplicationRumor at this point. Re-publishing ensures the new member receives the current profile in a live message that cannot be missed by history gaps.

invariants.api_contracts: subscribeToGroupMessages() signature unchanged; onMembersChanged parameter type (members: string[]) => void unchanged
invariants.existing_behavior: All profile publishing via onHistorySynced unchanged. Score sync, group storage, member list updates all unchanged. publishProfileUpdate() manual path unchanged.
invariants.performance: Only fires sendApplicationRumor when member count increases — not on every ingest event. No busy-loop or duplicate sends on stable membership.
invariants.backward_compatibility: No protocol changes. Profile message format identical to what onHistorySynced already sends. New members receive a duplicate profile message if they also trigger onHistorySynced on A's side — last-writer-wins by updatedAt handles deduplication.

fix_approach: In the subscribeNewGroups() for-loop, before calling subscribeToGroupMessages(), capture the initial member count in a closure-scoped variable: let prevMemberCount = mlsMembers.length. Then inside the onMembersChanged callback (currently lines 397-403), after updating storage, add: if (newMembers.length > prevMemberCount) { const payload = serialiseProfileUpdate(localProfile); const rumor = { kind: 1, content: payload, tags: [['t', 'quizzl-profile']], created_at: Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000), pubkey: pubkeyHex ?? '', id: '' }; void mlsGroup.sendApplicationRumor(rumor).catch(...); } prevMemberCount = newMembers.length;
constraints: Do not remove profilePublishedRef guard from onHistorySynced
Only republish when member count INCREASES — not on leave or stable membership

similar_patterns_fixed: none — this is the only onMembersChanged callback site

verification_criteria: onMembersChanged fires sendApplicationRumor when newMembers.length > prevMemberCount
onMembersChanged does NOT fire sendApplicationRumor when members leave (count decreases or stays same)
Regression test asserting member-join triggers profile republish PASSES
E2E groups-member-profiles.spec.ts 10s waitForTimeout workaround can eventually be removed
